name: pptx-posters
tagline: Builds AI-illustrated HTML research posters (with strict anti-clutter rules) exportable to PDF or PPTX via Chrome/LibreOffice.
verdict: Clean skill: no secret harvesting, no piped/remote code execution, no hidden instructions, and it clearly states when (and when not) to use itself. Minor rough edges: it doesn't say what to do if LibreOffice/Chrome are missing, and Stage 3's `cp poster_html_template.html poster.html` will silently overwrite an existing poster.html on a re-run. The optional O

highlights.summary: Content-quality failure modes (overflow, cramped text) are covered extensively (Quality Checklist, lines 325-367), but tooling failure paths are thin: 'Chrome headless PDF export... (if Chrome availab

Considerable repetition of the same constraints (3-4 elements, 50% white space, GIANT/MANDATORY/CRITICAL) across at least 5 sections (lines 52-90, 168-190, 325-367, 370-387) inflates context usage wit
